entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: A legume crop susceptible to aflatoxin contamination, particularly in subtropical environments

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS FLAVUS
entities.description: A fungal species of the Aspergillus genus that produces aflatoxins and infects peanut seed coats

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS PARASITICUS
entities.description: A fungal species of the Aspergillus genus that produces aflatoxins

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS NOMIUS
entities.description: A fungal species of the Aspergillus genus that produces aflatoxins

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS TAMARIE
entities.description: A fungal species of the Aspergillus genus that produces aflatoxins

entities.name: AFLATOXINS
entities.description: Toxic metabolites and group 1 carcinogens produced by certain Aspergillus species

entities.name: AFLATOXIN CONTAMINATION
entities.description: The presence of aflatoxins within the peanut crop, posing a major problem in subtropical environments

entities.name: SUBTROPICAL ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Environments where aflatoxin contamination in peanut crops is particularly prevalent

entities.name: MYCOTOXINS
entities.description: A class of toxins produced by fungi, of which aflatoxins are the most well-known

entities.name: GROUP 1 CARCINOGENS
entities.description: A classification of substances known to be carcinogenic to humans, including aflatoxins

entities.name: GROWTH DEFECTS
entities.description: Health issues in humans and livestock linked to aflatoxin exposure

entities.name: LIVER DISEASES
entities.description: Health issues in humans and livestock linked to aflatoxin exposure

entities.name: HUMANS
entities.description: Species affected by the carcinogenic and toxic effects of aflatoxins

entities.name: LIVESTOCK
entities.description: Animals affected by the carcinogenic and toxic effects of aflatoxins

entities.name: SEED COAT
entities.description: The outer layer of the peanut seed, also known as the testa, which acts as a defense barrier

entities.name: PLANT DEFENSE
entities.description: The mechanisms used by plants to protect themselves against pathogens

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS FLAVUS RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of the peanut plant to resist infection by Aspergillus flavus

entities.name: TESTA
entities.description: The botanical term for the seed coat of the peanut

entities.name: CELL WALL MATRIX
entities.description: A complex structure in the seed coat consisting of cellulose, lignin, hemicellulose, phenolic compounds, and structural proteins

entities.name: CELLULOSE
entities.description: A structural component of the peanut seed coat cell wall matrix

entities.name: LIGNIN
entities.description: A structural component of the peanut seed coat cell wall matrix

entities.name: HEMICELLULOSE
entities.description: A structural component of the peanut seed coat cell wall matrix

entities.name: PHENOLIC COMPOUNDS
entities.description: Chemical components of the seed coat cell wall matrix that contribute to defense

entities.name: STRUCTURAL PROTEINS
entities.description: Proteins that form part of the seed coat cell wall matrix

entities.name: CELL WALL DESICCATION
entities.description: The drying of cell walls during seed coat maturation, affecting how pathogens encounter resistance

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS FLAVUS INFECTION
entities.description: The process of Aspergillus flavus invading the peanut seed

entities.name: PHYSICAL AND BIOCHEMICAL BARRIER
entities.description: The role of the seed coat in preventing pathogen entry and growth

entities.name: POLYPHENOLIC COMPOUNDS
entities.description: Compounds in the seed coat cell walls that inhibit the growth of A. flavus and aflatoxin contamination

entities.name: PLANT BREEDING
entities.description: A strategy used to reduce A. flavus infection and aflatoxin contamination

entities.name: GENOMIC MECHANISMS
entities.description: Molecular approaches used to understand and reduce A. flavus infection

entities.name: MOLECULAR MECHANISMS
entities.description: Molecular approaches used to understand and reduce A. flavus infection

entities.name: MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: Agricultural practices used to reduce A. flavus infection and aflatoxin contamination

entities.name: SEED COAT CELL WALL-MEDIATED DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The specific resistance mechanism of the peanut seed coat cell wall against A. flavus and aflatoxins
